<div class="sphinxpress-doc">
<section id="jekyll-integration">
<h1>Jekyll integration</h1>
<p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">sphinxpress</span> <span class="pre">build-site</span></code> writes Markdown files with Jekyll front matter plus HTML rendered by Sphinx’s JSON builder.</p>
<p>The generated page front matter includes:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">layout</span></code></p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">title</span></code></p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">permalink</span></code></p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">nav_tool</span></code></p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">docs_project</span></code></p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">docs_variant</span></code></p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">docs_ref</span></code></p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">docs_commit</span></code></p></li>
</ul>
<p>Navigation data is written as YAML under the configured <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">site.nav_data_dir</span></code> path. A site layout can use <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">nav_tool</span></code> to select the matching navigation file.</p>
<p>When site versioning is enabled, each nav payload also includes:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">nav_key</span></code></p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">variant</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">variant_label</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">variant_kind</span></code></p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">source_ref</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">source_commit</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">source_url</span></code></p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">release_tag</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">release_url</span></code></p></li>
<li><p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">versions</span></code>, a version-switcher list for the logical project</p></li>
</ul>
<p>Generated files include a notice so they can be distinguished from hand-written site pages.</p>
<section id="scoped-api-stylesheet">
<h2>Scoped API stylesheet</h2>
<p>Every generated Jekyll page wraps the Sphinx body in a stable root container:</p>
<div class="highlight-html notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="p">&lt;</span><span class="nt">div</span> <span class="na">class</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s">&quot;sphinxpress-doc&quot;</span><span class="p">&gt;</span>...Sphinx body HTML...<span class="p">&lt;/</span><span class="nt">div</span><span class="p">&gt;</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>A small, scoped stylesheet is embedded as a <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">&lt;style</span> <span class="pre">data-sphinxpress-style=&quot;api&quot;&gt;</span></code> block immediately above the wrapper. The stylesheet:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>styles Python API descriptions, field lists, inline literals, and source links</p></li>
<li><p>uses CSS custom properties so host themes can recolor the panels</p></li>
<li><p>supports light and dark host themes via <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">prefers-color-scheme:</span> <span class="pre">dark</span></code></p></li>
<li><p>hides the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">[source]</span></code> link in <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">print</span></code> media</p></li>
</ul>
<p>The stylesheet is fully self-contained: it does not depend on Sphinx theme assets, fonts, or JavaScript, and it does not parse or rewrite the Sphinx signature HTML.</p>
<p>Host layouts should:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>preserve the <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">&lt;div</span> <span class="pre">class=&quot;sphinxpress-doc&quot;&gt;</span></code> wrapper</p></li>
<li><p>avoid overriding <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">.sphinxpress-doc</span> <span class="pre">dt.sig</span></code> with highly specific rules that would defeat the embedded style</p></li>
<li><p>rely on the embedded style for API presentation instead of shipping duplicate rules</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="liquid-protection">
<h2>Liquid protection</h2>
<p>Generated pages place the Sphinx HTML inside one sphinxpress-owned raw block
after front matter:</p>
<div class="highlight-text notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span>---
...
---

&lt;!-- GENERATED by sphinxpress. Do not edit by hand. --&gt;

{% raw %}
&lt;style ...&gt;...&lt;/style&gt;
&lt;div class=&quot;sphinxpress-doc&quot;&gt;...&lt;/div&gt;
&#123;% endraw %&#125;
</pre></div>
</div>
<p>This keeps Jekyll 3 from interpreting literal Liquid examples inside Sphinx
HTML. Literal <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">{%</span> <span class="pre">endraw</span> <span class="pre">%}</span></code> examples are neutralized before rendering so they
cannot terminate the outer raw block.</p>
</section>
<section id="version-switcher-layout">
<h2>Version switcher layout</h2>
<p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">sphinxpress/templates/tool-doc.html</span></code> is the canonical consumer layout for the
generated tool pages. It is shipped with the package (it is installed into
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">sphinxpress/templates/</span></code> alongside the other templates) and can be used in two
ways:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>Copy the file into the consuming site’s <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">_layouts/tool-doc.html</span></code> and let
sphinxpress keep generating pages that reference <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">layout:</span> <span class="pre">tool-doc</span></code>.</p></li>
<li><p>Or render the file directly from the package if the consuming site is willing
to point its <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">layout:</span></code> value at a vendored copy of the file.</p></li>
</ul>
<section id="minimal-front-matter">
<h3>Minimal front matter</h3>
<p>A generated tool page needs at least the following front matter to use the
layout (sphinxpress already writes this for every page, so consumer sites do
not have to add it manually):</p>
<div class="highlight-yaml notranslate"><div class="highlight"><pre><span></span><span class="nn">---</span>
<span class="nt">layout</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="l l-Scalar l-Scalar-Plain">tool-doc</span>
<span class="nt">title</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="s">&quot;sphinxpress&quot;</span>
<span class="nt">permalink</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="l l-Scalar l-Scalar-Plain">/tools/sphinxpress/</span>
<span class="nt">nav_tool</span><span class="p">:</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="l l-Scalar l-Scalar-Plain">sphinpress</span>
<span class="nn">---</span>
</pre></div>
</div>
<p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">nav_tool</span></code> is the only field the layout strictly requires. It selects the
matching entry from <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">site.data.tool_nav</span></code>. The other fields (<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">title</span></code>,
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">permalink</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">docs_project</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">docs_variant</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">docs_ref</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">docs_commit</span></code>) are
already documented above and can be passed through unchanged.</p>
</section>
<section id="what-the-layout-renders">
<h3>What the layout renders</h3>
<p>The layout reads <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">nav</span> <span class="pre">=</span> <span class="pre">site.data.tool_nav[page.nav_tool]</span></code> and renders:</p>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>the tool title as a link to the tool’s release-variant index page,</p></li>
<li><p>a <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">&lt;details</span> <span class="pre">class=&quot;tool-nav-versions&quot;&gt;</span></code> block sourced from <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">nav.versions</span></code>
(the entry with <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">current:</span> <span class="pre">true</span></code> is shown as plain text inside the
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">&lt;summary&gt;</span></code>, every other entry is rendered as a link to its own <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">url</span></code>),</p></li>
<li><p>the existing “View on GitHub” link from <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">nav.repo_url</span></code> (when present),</p></li>
<li><p>the existing “Latest release” link from <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">nav.release_tag</span></code> / <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">nav.release_url</span></code>
(when present),</p></li>
<li><p>the entries <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">&lt;ul&gt;</span></code> from <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">nav.entries</span></code>, with the existing label cleanup
(<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">entry.nav_title</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">entry.label</span></code>, <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">entry.slug</span> <span class="pre">==</span> <span class="pre">'index'</span> <span class="pre">-&gt;</span> <span class="pre">'Overview'</span></code>,
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">entry.title</span> <span class="pre">|</span> <span class="pre">remove_first:</span> <span class="pre">tool_prefix</span></code> fallback) and the existing
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">active</span></code> / <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">aria-current=&quot;page&quot;</span></code> rule keyed on <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">page.url</span> <span class="pre">==</span> <span class="pre">entry.url</span></code>.</p></li>
</ul>
<p>If <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">nav.versions</span></code> is missing or empty, the version switcher <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">&lt;details&gt;</span></code> block
is omitted entirely; the rest of the layout still renders normally. This keeps
the layout working for working-tree-only sites (the
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">example_site</span></code> fixture falls back cleanly) and for any future variant source
that does not yet populate <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">versions</span></code>.</p>
<p>The layout does not pull a “Latest release” hint or a “Source” link into the
switcher itself; release variants still show <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">release_tag</span></code> next to the title,
and the per-page front matter already carries the source ref and commit.</p>
</section>
</section>
</section>
</div>
<script data-sphinxpress-script="search" defer>
(function () {
  "use strict";

  function initForm(form) {
    var input = form.querySelector(".tool-search-input");
    var results = form.querySelector(".tool-search-results");
    var navList = form.parentElement
      ? form.parentElement.querySelector(".tool-nav-list")
      : null;
    if (!input || !results) {
      return;
    }
    var emptyText = form.getAttribute("data-search-empty") || "No matches.";
    var indexUrl = form.getAttribute("data-search-index");
    if (!indexUrl) {
      hideForm();
      return;
    }

    var indexEntries = null;
    var indexError = false;
    var pendingQuery = null;

    function hideForm() {
      form.style.display = "none";
    }

    function escapeHtml(value) {
      return value
        .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
        .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
        .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")
        .replace(/"/g, "&quot;")
        .replace(/'/g, "&#39;");
    }

    function showEmpty() {
      results.innerHTML =
        '<p class="tool-search-empty">' + escapeHtml(emptyText) + "</p>";
      results.hidden = false;
      if (navList) {
        navList.hidden = true;
      }
    }

    function clearResults() {
      results.innerHTML = "";
      results.hidden = true;
      if (navList) {
        navList.hidden = false;
      }
    }

    function renderResults(entries, tokens) {
      if (!entries.length) {
        showEmpty();
        return;
      }
      var html = entries
        .map(function (entry) {
          var snippetHtml = renderSnippet(entry, tokens);
          var href = entry.url + "#sphinxpress-search-hit";
          return (
            '<li class="tool-search-result">' +
            '<a href="' +
            escapeHtml(href) +
            '">' +
            '<p class="tool-search-result-title">' +
            escapeHtml(entry.title) +
            "</p>" +
            '<p class="tool-search-result-snippet">' +
            snippetHtml +
            "</p>" +
            "</a>" +
            "</li>"
          );
        })
        .join("");
      results.innerHTML = html;
      results.hidden = false;
      if (navList) {
        navList.hidden = true;
      }
    }

    function renderSnippet(entry, tokens) {
      var source = entry.snippet || "";
      if (!source) {
        return "";
      }
      var lower = source.toLowerCase();
      var firstToken = tokens[0] || "";
      var idx = lower.indexOf(firstToken);
      if (idx < 0) {
        return escapeHtml(source);
      }
      var before = source.slice(0, idx);
      var match = source.slice(idx, idx + firstToken.length);
      var after = source.slice(idx + firstToken.length);
      return (
        escapeHtml(before) +
        "<mark>" +
        escapeHtml(match) +
        "</mark>" +
        escapeHtml(after)
      );
    }

    function runQuery(query) {
      if (!indexEntries) {
        return;
      }
      var tokens = query
        .toLowerCase()
        .split(/\s+/)
        .filter(function (token) {
          return token.length > 0;
        });
      if (!tokens.length) {
        clearResults();
        return;
      }
      var hits = [];
      for (var i = 0; i < indexEntries.length; i += 1) {
        var entry = indexEntries[i];
        if (!entry.sections || !entry.sections.length) {
          continue;
        }
        var matched = true;
        var score = 0;
        for (var t = 0; t < tokens.length; t += 1) {
          var token = tokens[t];
          var found = false;
          var tokenScore = 0;
          for (var s = 0; s < entry.sections.length; s += 1) {
            var section = entry.sections[s];
            var lowerText = section.lower || "";
            if (!lowerText) {
              continue;
            }
            var from = 0;
            while (from <= lowerText.length) {
              var foundAt = lowerText.indexOf(token, from);
              if (foundAt < 0) {
                break;
              }
              tokenScore += 1;
              from = foundAt + token.length;
            }
            if (tokenScore > 0) {
              found = true;
            }
          }
          if (!found) {
            matched = false;
            break;
          }
          score += tokenScore;
        }
        if (matched) {
          hits.push({ entry: entry, score: score });
        }
      }
      hits.sort(function (a, b) {
        return b.score - a.score;
      });
      var top = hits.slice(0, 20).map(function (hit) {
        return hit.entry;
      });
      renderResults(top, tokens);
    }

    function handleInput() {
      var query = input.value || "";
      if (!query.trim()) {
        clearResults();
        pendingQuery = null;
        return;
      }
      if (indexError) {
        hideForm();
        return;
      }
      if (indexEntries) {
        runQuery(query);
      } else {
        pendingQuery = query;
      }
    }

    input.addEventListener("input", handleInput);

    fetch(indexUrl, { credentials: "same-origin" })
      .then(function (response) {
        if (!response.ok) {
          throw new Error("HTTP " + response.status);
        }
        return response.json();
      })
      .then(function (payload) {
        if (
          !payload ||
          !Array.isArray(payload.entries) ||
          payload.entries.length === 0
        ) {
          hideForm();
          return;
        }
        indexEntries = payload.entries;
        if (pendingQuery) {
          runQuery(pendingQuery);
        }
      })
      .catch(function () {
        indexError = true;
        if (input.value && input.value.trim()) {
          hideForm();
        } else {
          hideForm();
        }
      });
  }

  function init() {
    var forms = document.querySelectorAll("form.tool-search");
    for (var i = 0; i < forms.length; i += 1) {
      initForm(forms[i]);
    }
  }

  if (document.readyState === "loading") {
    document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", init);
  } else {
    init();
  }
})();

</script>
{% endraw %}
